zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Bowes於大約1503年出生在英國達勒姆澤亞哈姆時，他的父親Sir Ralph Bowes51歲，母親Margaret Conyers36歲。他於1521年7月8日與Elizabeth Aske結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1558年11月10日在英國達勒姆享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Bowes于大约1503年出生在英国達勒姆澤亞哈姆时，他的父亲Sir Ralph Bowes51岁，母亲Margaret Conyers36岁。他于1521年7月8日与Elizabeth Aske结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1558年11月10日在英国達勒姆享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bowes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from Bowes (formerly in North Yorkshire, now in County Durham), or from some other place so called, the placename being derived from the plural of Old English boga ‘bow’, here referring to bends in a river. Alternatively, a topographic name for someone who lived ‘(at the) arches or bridge’. See Bow 1.
English: variant of Bow , with excrescent -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Buadhaigh (see Bogue ).

variants.name: Bow
meanings: Scottish and English: habitational name from any of various minor places called with Old English boga ‘bow, arch, vault’, i.e. an arched bridge. There are places so called in Midlothian, Middlesex, and Devon.
Irish and English: variant of   Bowes  .

Chinese: possibly from Cantonese form of the Chinese names 寶 (meaning ‘treasure’) or 保 (meaning ‘protect’), which were monosyllabic personal names or part of disyllabic personal names of some early Chinese immigrants in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Bowes was born about 1503, in Seaham, Durham, England, his father, Sir Ralph Bowes, was 51 and his mother, Margaret Conyers, was 36. He married Elizabeth Aske on 8 July 1521. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 10 November 1558, in Durham, England, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Bowes est né environ 1503, à Seaham, Durham, Angleterre, son père, Sir Ralph Bowes, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Margaret Conyers, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Aske le 8 juillet 1521. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 10 novembre 1558, à Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Bowesが約1503年にイギリスダルハムセアハムで生まれた時，父Sir Ralph Bowesは51歳，母Margaret Conyersは36歳であった。1521年7月8日にElizabeth Askeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1558年11月10日にイギリスダルハムで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Bowes(이)가 대략 1503년 영국 뒤람 시함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Ralph Bowes(은)는 51세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Conyers(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1521년 7월 8일 Elizabeth Aske(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1558년 11월 10일 영국 뒤람에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Bowes nasceu aproximadamente 1503, em Seaham, Durham, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Ralph Bowes, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Conyers, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Aske em 8 de julho de 1521. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de novembro de 1558, em Durham, Inglaterra, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Bowes родился около 1503 г. в местности сихам, дурам, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Ralph Bowes было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Conyers, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Aske 8 июля 1521 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 10 ноября 1558 г. в местности дурам, Англия в возрасте 56 лет.
